purpose: Golden set for the meaning-based preference-contradiction feasibility study. Each pair is two operator preferences. `should_flag` = the M2 near-duplicate/contradiction confirm flow SHOULD surface a 'this may replace X - confirm?' prompt (the new preference targets the same standing dimension as the existing one, whether by paraphrase-equivalence or by opposite value). Positives are deliberately LOW word-overlap so the production deterministic Jaccard probe (PREFERENCE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD=0.6) misses them - that miss is the gap the meaning-based signal must close. Negatives split into HARD (high word overlap but a different/compatible dimension - the Jaccard false-alarm trap the embedding signal must NOT copy) and EASY (unrelated).
matcher_semantics_note: The M2 signal is a SAME-DIMENSION detector, not a logical-contradiction classifier. For the advisory confirm flow, a paraphrase-equivalent pair and an opposite-value pair should BOTH prompt the operator (both mean 'you may be replacing an existing rule'). Embedding cosine measures topical proximity, which is exactly this notion - it deliberately conflates paraphrase and negation, and that conflation is correct for the use case.

classes.contradiction: should_flag=true; low word overlap; subclass paraphrase_equiv | meaning_contradiction | negation
classes.hard_negative: should_flag=false; HIGH word overlap, different or compatible dimension

pairs.b: address me by my first name
pairs.note: same dimension (form of address), equivalent, low overlap

pairs.b: give temperatures on the metric scale
pairs.note: Celsius is metric - equivalent, low overlap

pairs.b: be concise in your responses
pairs.note: same brevity dimension, no shared content word
